Workspace

Building & floor structure, appearance, and defaults for newly-added units.

Current Building & Floor
—
Floors
Appearance
Defaults for new units
When a unit is resized (or a floor calibration changes), capacity auto-updates to floor(sqft / value). Typical: 30 ft²/person (US OSHA min for office), 50 (comfortable), 75 (coworking).

Automate the financial side: when invoices go out, what late fees accrue, and what enforcement kicks in.

Late Fee Policy
Workspace-wide rule — applies to every active lease unless overridden per unit. Pre-filled to match SuitesForAll standard lease clause §5 (8% / 5d grace / monthly).
📄 Standard lease clause §5 — Late Fees (reference)
Any undisputed amounts not paid by the 5th calendar day of the month are automatically subject to a one-time fee of 8% of the total outstanding balance. The fee applies once per month of delinquency, is calculated on the full unpaid balance (rent + taxes + services), and is due immediately with the principal debt. Rejected / canceled / incomplete payments are not received. Provider may require guaranteed payment (wire / cashier's check) after any late event and apply additional measures including access suspension.
%
Post-late enforcement
📊 Sample calculation
—
Auto-invoicing
Scheduled Cloud Function sends each occupied unit's monthly rent invoice on the configured day. Per-unit opt-out lives on the unit's detail panel.
E.g. 10 → April invoices go out on Mar 22 (31-day Mar) or Mar 22 (30-day Apr-1 minus 10) — automatically adjusts for month length.
Tenant must pay within this window (Stripe due_date). Match this to "before days" so invoice falls due exactly on the 1st.
Auto-charge after first payment
Enable this to have Stripe automatically charge the tenant's saved payment method on subsequent invoices. The first invoice is still sent normally — when the tenant pays, their card is saved as the default. From then on, invoices bill automatically on the due date, no tenant action required.
⚠ Legal note: Auto-charging requires explicit tenant authorization. In most US states your lease must contain an ACH/auto-debit clause. When the tenant pays their first invoice through Stripe's hosted page, they implicitly authorize storing the payment method — but review your jurisdiction's rules first.
Additional services

Workspace catalog — services and prices that apply to NEW leases. Existing tenants keep the prices they signed up for; you can view those via the unit's ⋯ menu → Additional services.

Add service

Define a billable add-on the tenant pays on top of base rent.

Connect external services. Disconnected integrations show a red dot on this tab.

✨
AI Room Detection
Choose a vision AI provider
Ready
OpenRouter is a gateway to GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini via one key. Get yours at openrouter.ai/keys.
S
Stripe (Tenant Payments)
Accept rent payments online
Not connected
The backend (Cloud Functions) is live — invoices, reminders, reconcile, and webhooks all run server-side. These UI fields only mirror what's in your Firebase Secrets for reference; rotate keys via firebase functions:secrets:set STRIPE_SECRET_KEY.
💡 Auto-invoicing & late fees moved to Settings → Billing.
🏦
Bank Connections (Bank Feed)
Pull deposits from your business bank · Capital One / Chase / BofA / etc.
Not connected
Powered by Stripe Financial Connections. Connect your business bank account once via OAuth — read-only access to incoming transactions. Used to auto-match wire / ACH / check deposits to open rent invoices. Cost: $1.50 per connect + $0.10/mo per active account. Requires the publishable key in the Stripe section above.
Phase 1 (current): connect & list accounts. Phase 2: scheduled transaction poll. Phase 3: auto-match to invoices. Phase 4: operator review board.
D
DocuSign (E-Sign Leases)
Send leases for electronic signature — OAuth PKCE, browser-direct
Not connected
Create a Single-Page App with PKCE in DocuSign Admin → Apps and Keys. Enable CORS for this origin and "Allow CORS for OAuth calls".
Inline mode (recommended, default): leave this blank. The app generates a full, professionally formatted lease on every send, with all values (tenant, suite, rent, dates, addresses) already typed into the flowing prose. Text reflows naturally so long company names or addresses never clip. DocuSign only collects the Client's signature + signing date.

Template mode (advanced): paste a DocuSign Template UUID here to use a custom template with positional tabs. The template must declare a Client role and 9 Text Data Labels (client_company, tenant_name, building_address, suite, rent, deposit, lease_start, lease_end, term). Only use this if you need a legally reviewed document you can't edit in code.
Workflow defaults (expiration, reminders, email template, hard-gate)
These settings are shared across the workspace (synced to every admin). Email subject and body support {name} {suite} {rent} {building} placeholders.
L
Lease Defaults
Policy values, fee schedule & jurisdiction — merged into every lease the app sends
Phase 1
These values populate merge fields in your DocuSign lease template (provider_legal_name, late_fee_pct, price_bw_copy, etc.). Change a value here and the next lease sent will carry the new value. Leave a field blank to use the built-in default.
Provider Info (who you are, legally)
Jurisdiction (governing law & venue for disputes)
Financial Terms (late fees, interest, notice periods, liability)
Price Guide (Exhibit C line items — copies, cards, rekey, etc.)
🔑
Access Control — UniFi Access
Auto-issue door credentials (NFC, mobile tap, PIN) when tenants are added
Phase 1a
Point this at your UniFi Access controller. Define door groups (which readers open for whom), and the app will surface an Access tab on every unit for issuing / revoking credentials. In Phase 1a the actions are simulated locally; in Phase 1b (Firebase Functions) they start hitting the real controller.
Phase 1b wires this to Firebase Functions. The API token never touches this form — it goes to Secret Manager.
Door groups (which readers a credential opens)
Phase 1b brings the Firebase Functions backend that actually calls the UniFi API (provision / revoke / sync events). Phase 3 adds Apple / Google Wallet pass signing.
📄
Employment Contract
One PDF template for all employees · individual signed copies per person
Phase 3a
Upload the standard employment agreement (PDF). When you send a contract to an employee from their edit dialog, this template is used — merged with their name, hire date, role, and address.
Loading…
Phase 3b adds DocuSign envelope generation with merge fields (name, address, SSN, hire date) so employees sign in-app instead of via manual upload.
G
Google Drive (Cloud Storage)
Store photos & documents beyond browser limits
Phase 2
Requires OAuth setup. Create credentials at Google Cloud Console and paste the OAuth Client ID below.
Activating cloud storage needs Phase 2 backend (for refresh token handling).
⚡
Firebase (Real-time Collaboration)
Multiple agents editing simultaneously
Phase 2
Create a free project at Firebase Console and paste the web config below.
○ Not syncing
State pushes to users/{uid} ~1s after each edit. Open the same URL on another device to see it mirror live.
Gmail (Google Workspace)
Send notice letters and tenant correspondence from your own Gmail address. Letters appear in your Sent folder.
— Not connected
Click Connect Gmail below — Google will ask permission to send email on your behalf. We never see your password. After connecting, a 📧 Send via Gmail button appears wherever the app generates outbound letters.

Default lease templates for your workspace. Each unit can also have its own override (set from the unit's Lease tab → Edit template).

Document templates

Pick a type below to view / edit the workspace default. Changes apply to every new document of that type — unless a specific unit has its own override.

Common charges

Pre-defined damage / cleaning / penalty line items you can add to any move-out balance with one click. Useful for recurring charges like "Damaged carpet" or "Lost key fob".

Add charge

Define a reusable charge line item. Pick it from the Move-out modal to add to any tenant's balance.
You can override this amount when picking the charge in a specific case.

Backups, archived units, and full export / import of workspace state.

One-time backfills
Mark all pre-existing tenants' deposits as already paid (they paid outside this system before it tracked Stripe). Only tenants added today are skipped — those go through the normal Stripe flow. New tenants going forward stamp automatically when Stripe webhooks confirm payment.
Clean phantom backfills — removes "Backfill (migrated)" payment records dated BEFORE each tenant's lease start. Use after running the original backfill on tenants with short tenancies (otherwise their ledger shows fictitious "paid" months from before they moved in). Real, manually-entered payments are not touched.
Local backups in this browser
Captured every page load (before any code runs on your data) + every 30 minutes during the session. Lives in localStorage — works offline, restores in < 1 second. Console: sfaBackup(), sfaListBackups(), sfaRestore('key'), sfaBackupHelp().
Loading…
Server backups via Firestore
Server-side: 15-minute snapshots (48-hour retention) + daily snapshots at 03:00 UTC (90-day retention). Restore creates a pre-restore safety snapshot first. Backups live in /workspaces/default/backups/.
No backups yet.
Audit log
Every payment status change, invoice send, restore, and bulk operation is logged here. Use to answer "who changed this and when?". Pending entries (yellow badge above) are queued locally and retry every 30s — survives reload.
Click Refresh to load recent audit entries.
Archive
Archived units are hidden from all active tables but their payment history and Stripe links stay intact. Restore anytime.
No archived units.
Data Management

Reference documentation for how the system encodes invoices, schedules auto-invoicing, and handles late fees. Updated whenever the underlying logic changes.

Invoice numbering
Auto-invoicing

The system can automatically send each occupied unit's monthly rent invoice on a schedule you configure.

Workspace default (Settings → Billing):

  • Enable auto-invoicing — master on/off switch
  • Send N days before 1st of next month — handles different month lengths automatically (Feb → Mar: 1 Mar − N; Jun → Jul: 1 Jul − N)
  • Due days after send — Stripe due_date on the invoice. Set to match the "before days" so it falls due exactly on the 1st

Per-unit override (Unit panel → Finance → Auto-invoicing):

  • This unit dropdown: Inherit workspace default / Always auto-invoice / Never (manual only)
  • Send (days before 1st): leave empty to inherit, or set a per-unit override (1-28)

Immediate first invoice: when you flip a unit's auto-invoice setting on (from off/inherit-off), the system fires the next-month rent invoice right away — no waiting for the scheduled cron to come around.

Idempotency: for each unit, only one auto-invoice per billing month. If already sent (stamped on u.stripe.autoSentYm) or already paid, the cron skips that unit.

Auto-charge (auto-debit after first payment)

Stripe can automatically debit the tenant's saved card/bank on the due date, so you don't rely on them remembering to pay each month.

How it works:

  • First invoice is sent normally as a hosted Stripe page (collection_method: send_invoice) with save_default_payment_method: on_confirmation. When the tenant pays, their card is saved as the Stripe customer's default payment method.
  • Subsequent invoices are created with collection_method: charge_automatically. Stripe auto-charges the saved card on the due date without tenant action.
  • Failed charges trigger Stripe's built-in Smart Retries (up to 4 attempts over ~3 weeks with exponential backoff). If all retries fail, the invoice moves to past_due and your unit gets the red "!" overdue marker. Operator gets an email notification (via webhook).

Config levels:

  • Workspace: Settings → Billing → "Enable auto-charge workspace-wide" (default off)
  • Per-unit: Unit panel → Finance → Auto-invoicing → "Auto-charge card" dropdown (Inherit / Always / Never)

Legal note: Auto-charging requires explicit tenant authorization. Check your lease has an ACH/auto-debit clause before enabling. When the tenant pays the first invoice through Stripe's hosted page and consents to saving their card, that acts as explicit authorization — but verify your jurisdiction's rules.

Late fees

When a rent invoice goes unpaid past its grace period, late fees can be accrued and billed on a separate invoice.

  • Grace days — days after the 1st before a late fee starts accruing (typical: 5)
  • Fee type — flat dollar amount OR percent of rent (default: 8%)
  • Apply to — rent only, or rent + taxes + services (total balance)
  • Frequency — once per overdue cycle, or monthly recurring until paid

Dismissal: operators can waive an individual month's fee from the Late-fee block on the Overview tab. Dismissed fees don't count toward outstanding.

Deposit handling

Deposits are separate from rent — one-time up-front charges tracked on their own invoice (D- prefix) and stored on u.stripe.depositInvoice.

  • Default amount — 1 month's rent (auto-mirrors when editing rent, can be overridden)
  • Not included in Outstanding stat — deposit is shown separately on its own "Deposit" row
  • One-time backfill (Settings → Data) — mark all pre-existing tenants' deposits as paid retroactively. Auto-fires on first app load per workspace (gated by settings.migrations.depositBackfillV1)
Status badges on the floor plan

Each occupied unit shows up to 3 corner pills indicating lease and invoice status. Visible for 30 days after tenant onboarding, then hide for a clean map.

  • L (top-right corner group) — lease: green = signed, grey = awaiting, transparent = not sent
  • $ rent (bottom-left corner group) — current month rent invoice
  • $ deposit (bottom-right corner group) — security deposit invoice
  • Pill colors — green #16a34a = paid, grey #6b7280 = open/sent, muted #94a3b8 at 35% = not sent yet

Red "!" circle (bottom-right corner): this unit has overdue rent. Click to open Finance tab for amount details.

Cache persistence: pill colors are persisted to localStorage (sfa_inv_buckets_v1) so they render immediately on page reload — no grey placeholder while Stripe list loads.

✂️ Crop Floor Plan

Drag the selection to move it. Drag corners to resize. Only the area inside the blue rectangle will be kept.

🔗 Reconcile Stripe Invoices

Walks every invoice on your Stripe account and tries to match each one to a unit in your portfolio. Matches are made by: app metadata → tenant email → suite number in description.

💸 Late Fee Policy

Workspace-wide defaults — apply to every active lease automatically. Per-unit overrides (in the unit's Late fee policy section) win when set.
📄 Standard lease clause §5 — Late Fees (reference)
Any undisputed amounts not paid by the 5th calendar day of the month are automatically subject to a one-time fee of 8% of the total outstanding balance. The fee:
  • applies once per month of delinquency
  • is calculated on the full unpaid balance (rent + taxes + services)
  • is due immediately with the principal debt
Payment is considered received only when funds actually clear. Rejected, canceled, or incomplete payments are not received. Provider may require guaranteed payment (wire / cashier's check) after any late event and apply additional measures including access suspension.
Enable late fees
When off, no fees are computed or surfaced anywhere.
Grace period
Days into the month before a fee accrues. Contract default: 5.
days
Fee type
Amount
Contract default: 8%.
%
Apply to
What the % is calculated against.
Frequency
Per contract: one fee per delinquent month.
Post-late enforcement
Optional restrictions triggered after the first late event.

📥 Import bank statement

Add transactions from a file when Stripe doesn't reach far enough back. Supports CSV, Excel (xlsx/xls), OFX, QFX, and QBO (QuickBooks). Days that already have transactions in the system are skipped automatically to prevent overlap with the bank feed.
📂
Drop a statement file here, or click to browse
Accepted: .csv · .xlsx · .xls · .ofx · .qfx · .qbo (max 5 MB)

If your file has a single signed Amount column, leave Credit/Debit on "(none)". Otherwise use the split columns.

Workspace default applies to all suites
Edits create a Suite-specific override that applies to all future lease documents for this unit.

🪄 Auto-Detect Rooms

Scans the uploaded floor plan for rooms (light areas surrounded by walls) and proposes unit rectangles. Adjust sensitivity if results look wrong.
Detection Method
Note on AI Vision: Current vision LLMs (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini) struggle with precise spatial localization on complex floor plans. They often produce "plausible-looking" uniform grids instead of real rooms. The Local Algorithm uses classical computer vision (Otsu + morphological closing + flood fill) — tested to 103% accuracy on synthetic 60-room plans.
⚠ No AI provider connected. Open Settings → AI Room Detection →
Local Algorithm Settings
Tries threshold (Otsu) + multiple closing radii (3, 5, 8, 12, 18, 25), picks the combination that finds the most rooms. Works for simple floor plans out of the box.
Manual overrides (used only when Auto-tune is OFF):
150
12px
25px
80
